dismissalText.commentary: Rohit Sharma c Dube b Ahmed 0 (4b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: Softie! Taken at midwicket! Rohit flicked the 139ks length ball just in front of the body. Wasn't an aggressive shot. Line was middle-stumpish, so the shot was on. There was no swing either, the bat face didn't really close. So, Rohit might have wanted to hit that straight. Ended up lobbing it to Dube.

dismissalText.commentary: Will Jacks c Dube b Ashwin 11 (7b 2x4 0x6 19m) SR: 157.14

dismissalComment.html: Jacks holes out to extra cover, big smile on Ashwin's face as he high-fives Dhoni. Was a carrom ball that seemed to swing in, finished around top of middle. Jacks tried a powerplay shot, just lift it over the infield while in the crease. Playing on the up didn't work out. Off the bottom of the bat, Dube hobbles back a couple of steps and takes a simple catch.

dismissalText.commentary: Suryakumar Yadav st †Dhoni b Noor Ahmad 29 (26b 2x4 1x6 41m) SR: 111.53

dismissalComment.html: age 43 but reflexes of a 23-year old! Dhoni has whipped the bails off in a jiffy like he's been doing that for 30 years. He probably has. Noor turns this one away from SKY, he ventures out of the crease to drive down the ground and doesn't get close at all, and Dhoni takes the bails off in a flash. Easy decision for the third umpire

dismissalText.commentary: Tilak Varma lbw b Noor Ahmad 31 (25b 2x4 2x6 41m) SR: 124

dismissalComment.html: trapped right in front and given straightaway! Another one bites the dust and this is a big wicket. That is plumb unless Tilak has edged it, and he has reviewed it. UltraEdge on the screen now, flat line as ball passes the bat, and ball-tracking is taking time. Here it is, turning in from outside off, impact in line, going on to hit middle and that's three reds!

dismissalText.commentary: Naman Dhir b Noor Ahmad 17 (12b 1x4 0x6 18m) SR: 141.66

dismissalComment.html: bowled around his legs and Naman Dhir can barely believe it! Fourth wicket for Noor Ahmad and what a way to get that fourth. Pitches it just outside leg, Dhir goes for the sweep, misses the sweep, and it's the wrong'un that goes on to rattle leg stump. Dhir looks back at the stumps and then towards the bowler. Did that really happen?

dismissalText.commentary: Mitchell Santner lbw b Ellis 11 (13b 1x4 0x6 24m) SR: 84.61

dismissalComment.html: 135.2kmh, this ain't a slower one as Santner misses this one on the stumps, appeal for lbw and CSK are interested in this. Is it high? Because it was a short of length ball. They review the not out decision. No bat there, and there you go - ball-tracking says that's hitting top of middle! Excellent review
